name: Amaretto Cheesecake with Raspberry Sauce
recipeIngredient: Cream cheese (for filling): 12 oz (375 g), softened
Amaretto or other almond liqueur (for filling): 2 tbsp
recipeInstructions:
Base:
1. Melt butter in a microwavable quiche dish, shallow round baking dish, or deep pie plate at high power for 1 to 1 1/2 minutes.
2. Stir in almonds and sugar until evenly coated with butter.
3. Press into the bottom and sides of the dish.
4. Microwave at high for 2 to 3 minutes or until firm. If necessary, rotate the dish during cooking.
Filling:
5. Beat cream cheese and granulated sugar together until light.
6. Add eggs one at a time, then sour cream, Amaretto or other almond liqueur, vanilla, and almond extract.
7. Mix until smooth.
8. Pour into the baked crust.
9. Microwave uncovered at medium power for 14 to 18 minutes or until the cheesecake is almost set in the center. Rotate the dish halfway through cooking if necessary.
Sauce:
10. Reserve some whole raspberries for garnish.
11. Puree the remaining raspberries and push through a sieve to remove seeds.
12. Stir 2 tablespoons of Amaretto or other almond liqueur into the puree.
13. Add sugar to taste.
To Serve:
14. Spoon some sauce onto dessert plates.
15. Place a slice of cheesecake on top of the sauce.
16. Garnish with reserved berries.
Makes 6 to 8 servings.
If you prefer, use 1 cup graham cracker crumbs mixed with 1/2 cup finely chopped almonds for the crust. Pulse in a food processor until the nuts are an even consistency. You could also substitute strawberries for the raspberries or omit liqueur by increasing almond extract in the filling to 1 teaspoon and using just a drop in the raspberry puree.
